rules.description: Deny DELETE without WHERE clause — too dangerous
rules.message: DELETE without WHERE clause is not allowed. Add a WHERE condition or use TRUNCATE with explicit approval.

rules.description: TRUNCATE always requires explicit approval — wipes entire table
rules.message: TRUNCATE requires explicit human approval. This will delete ALL rows in the table.
DROP operations are irreversible and require explicit approval.

rules.description: ALTER on any table requires dry-run and approval (schema changes can lock tables)
rules.message: ALTER operations may cause table locks. Review the execution plan before proceeding.

rules.description: DELETE with WHERE requires dry-run to estimate impact
rules.message: DELETE with WHERE requires dry-run impact assessment.

rules.description: UPDATE without WHERE affects all rows — require approval
rules.message: UPDATE without WHERE clause will affect ALL rows in the table.
UPDATE requires dry-run to verify row impact.

rules.description: INSERT operations are allowed with dry-run
SELECT queries are read-only — always pass-through
